reasoning: Multiple converging structural factors make official referendum date announcement before July 1, 2025, virtually impossible: (1) Supreme Court ruling (Nov 2022) blocked unilateral Scottish Parliament action (search_0_result_2, search_0_result_3); (2) UK government maintains opposition to another referendum (search_1_result_1); (3) First Minister Swinney stated 5-year timeline to 2029 is "realistic" (search_1_result_6, search_1_summary); (4) SNP lost 39 seats in 2024 election and lacks clear mandate (search_1_result_1); (5) Public support collapsed: only 14% prioritize independence, with 54% opposed (search_1_summary, search_4_result_2); (6) Government focused on NHS, education, cost of living (search_1_summary); (7) Next Scottish Parliament election is May 2026, after resolution date (search_1_summary); (8) Only 2-month window between knowledge cutoff and resolution date insufficient for legal/political process. All evidence sources consistent that referendum will not be scheduled this soon.
